calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gregory Winslow (Litigation Counsel) and Judith Baer (Probate Attorney) for Kayoko Ruocco.
Chris Wenthur for Stephen Ruocco and Andre Ruocco (Co-Trustees of The Ralph Thomas Ruocco Trust.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the amount of extraordinary fees requested are reasonable and appropriate, explained services provided/work performed, spoke of delays by the San Diego Assessors Office, time/hours spent on settlement, pro bono hours provided by Mr. Winslow and Ms. Baer, the Court's responsibility to oversee/review fees, who benefitted from administration of the Estate, the difference in the amounts received by the Beneficiaries, etc. The Second and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition To Approve Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-22-2017 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory Winslow for (administrator) Kayoko Ruocco. Ralph Hughes (via court call) for (beneficiaries) Stephen Ruocco & Ralph Ruocco.

The Court stated that it has read the papers. Mr. Hughes stated that his reading of the responses are that all alleged problems have been resolved, that the deeds have to be signed, that the CPA can complete the accounting by August 30, 2016 and that it seems that the estate will soon be in condition to close. Mr. Winslow stated that he agrees, that they have still not been paid by North American, that he does not believe that the accounting can be completed by August 30, 2016, that North American is dragging their feet, that they are waiting for them to prepare the transfer of title, that the property taxes have been paid, that they think all is clear on the Lot H issue, that things are wrapping up, that there are variables that are beyond time control, that they are asking for an additional 6 months but do not think they will really need that much time. Mr. Hughes stated that if the court orders the petition for final distribution to be filed by September 30, 2016 it would not get a hearing date until November, that that should be plenty of time, that the close was ordered in 2014 and it is now 2 years later. The Court took the Probate Accounting status reports under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Denied

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-09-2016 Continued To Aug-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition To Approve Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jul-13-2016 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory Winslow for (administrator) Kayoko Ruocco. Ralph Hughes (via court call) for (beneficiaries) Stephen Ruocco & Ralph Ruocco.

The Court stated that the administrator wishes to have approval of an order but the court does not have the accounting. Mr. Winslow stated that it was an inappropriate prayer, that the estate is waiting for the San Diego assessor to complete work on some exchanges & transfers, that he spoke with the assessor and is told that they are in the final states (but stated that the first 2 stages took 2 years) and requested a 6 month continuance. Mr. Hughes stated that there is a lot of money in the estate, that there could be a preliminary distribution, that the problem is a small lot in San Diego, that it should either be solved or cut away and that to keep continuing the matter is not acceptable. The Court stated that it has read the papers and reviewed the file, that the administrator is asking for 6 months to complete the accounting and that it is uncomfortable with the other requests. Mr. Winslow stated that they would like the continuance to either file the final accounting or a petition for approval, that there are 17 lots in San Diego, that there have been numerous transfers and that one lot in particular is a problem. The Court took the probate accounting status report under submission.

Judge: Charlene Kiesselbach. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Cho. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Continued To Jan-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition To Approve Report On Status Of Administration.
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition To Approve Report On Status Of Administration.
Off Calendar Jul-21-2015 Continued To Nov-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greg Winslow for (Administrator) Kayoko Ruocco. Laurie Barber (via court call) for (beneficiary) Stephen Ruocco.

Mr. Winslow reported that there are issues with the assessors office in Santee California, that there are numerous parcels, that it is confusing, that they are unable to obtain necessary information, that an error in a previous transfer was uncovered, that they were promised information by 6/30/15 but did not receive it, that Ms. Baer has called the assessors office and there is now no estimate on when they will receive the information, that money has been spent tracking down the new parcel, that the heirs in San Diego will be filing a claim against North American Title regarding the fees incurred and requested a 6 month continuance stating that they are prepared to move forward as soon as they get the information. Ms. Barber stated that this is not an issue for the estate, that title insurance was purchased and that the company is on the hook for everything, that it is not appropriate for the administrator to be chasing down the information, that there is an issue of $15,000.00 of her clients money in a trust account and not being returned and that he is having to pay interest on a loan that generated that money. Mr. Winslow stated that the $15,000.00 is subject to court approval in the final distribution and that they do not know what the taxes will be until they straighten out everything with the assessors office. The Court stated that the issue of the $15,000.00 is not before the court today. Mr. Winslow stated that they had believed that all properties were distributed and then the assessor came up with an additional lot. Ms. Barber stated that the $15,000.00 was approved last year and needs to be returned, that it was part of the settlement agreement, that all of the properties are valued at less than $3,000.00 and that it should not hold up the final petition. The Court stated that the settlement agreement is not before the court and that the parties apparently disagree on what it says. The status on filing of petition for final distribution was continued to November 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-22-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Judith Baer for Kayoko Ruocco and specially appearing for Attorney Brian Chikowski for KKayoko Ruocco as an Heir. Greg Winslow Court appointed litigation counsel for the Administrator. Constance Klein for Ralph Thomas Ruocco and Stephen Ruocco.

The Court stated that it read the petition and the settlement agreement and that it sounds as if the Parties resolved a number of issue at great expense to the estate. The accounting petition and the petition to approve settlement agreement were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
